danger_signals.visual_form: Cookies accumulating from top and right sides of playing field, filling grid beyond capacity
danger_signals.meaning: Game over condition; player has failed to clear cookies fast enough

danger_signals.visual_form: Progressive vertical fill of grid space as new cookies enter from top
danger_signals.meaning: Increasing time pressure and difficulty; imminent failure if not cleared

safety_signals.visual_form: Matching cookies of same type disappearing from horizontal or vertical line
safety_signals.meaning: Successful puzzle solution; progress toward level completion

safety_signals.visual_form: Active cursor on grid that can rotate lines of cookies
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and control; ability to manipulate board state

safety_signals.visual_form: Cookie shaped like Yoshi's head appearing occasionally on grid
safety_signals.meaning: Helper mechanism; can match any cookie type to clear lines

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Yoshi head-shaped cookie; Yoshi character branding in logo and title screen
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Logo with Yoshi character; Japanese version includes two sets of eyes in cloud; international version replaces with Yoshi Egg in orange box
Game developed as Hermetica arcade title, licensed and rebranded by Nintendo with Mario characters for NES/Game Boy versions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Grid partially filled with fewer cookie types; slower cookie descent speed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player completes successive levels with progressive complexity increase

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Grid filling rapidly; more cookie types on screen simultaneously
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Faster cookie entry from top and right; increased pressure to clear matching lines quickly

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Split-screen display with two independent grids side-by-side
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players compete; successful matches by one player can cause disruptions to opponent's grid (game mechanics enable player-to-player interference)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Predefined static grid layout rather than dynamically filling board
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must solve puzzle within maximum number of moves; no time pressure from cookie overflow

environmental_storytelling.example: Japanese logo features Yoshi with expressive eyes in large cloud; international logo redesigned with Yoshi Egg in orange box, clouds redrawn—communicates localization and market-specific branding rather than narrative world-state
Multiple cookie types on grid (each type visually distinct) communicate difficulty progression; more types present = harder puzzle

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Continuously accepts new cookies from top and right edges; responds to player cursor rotations; clears matching rows/columns upon completion
Acts as universal match; player can use it to complete any row or column regardless of other cookie types
Rotates individual lines of cookies (similar to Rubik's Cube mechanics); positions indicate available moves and strategic options

notes: Yoshi's Cookie is a tile-matching puzzle game with minimal narrative or faction conflict elements. World-state communication is entirely mechanical/gameplay-based: grid fill rate indicates difficulty progression; cookie overflow signals failure state. The game's visual identity derives from Mario/Yoshi IP branding rather than environmental storytelling. Development notes indicate the game originated as arcade title 'Hermetica' before being licensed and rebranded. No dynamic world states, political situations, or faction relationships beyond IP association. Manifestation layer is primarily about clarity of puzzle mechanics and difficulty signaling through visual density and accumulation.
sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor: Yoshi's Cookie (NES)
